seoTitle: How to Add a Location Link in a Canva Invite
metaDescription: Learn how to add a clickable location link to a Canva invitation so guests can find the venue with a single tap.
intro: Adding a location link to a digital invitation makes it easy for guests to pull up directions instantly, which is especially helpful for weddings, parties, and corporate events. This guide covers linking text or an icon to a maps URL inside Canva.

steps.heading: Add or select the venue text
steps.body: Highlight the address text or icon on your invitation that you want to make clickable.

steps.heading: Open the link tool
steps.body: Click the link icon in the toolbar once your text or element is selected.

steps.heading: Paste a maps URL
steps.body: Copy the venue's Google Maps (or similar) link and paste it into the link field.

steps.heading: Apply and confirm the link
steps.body: Save the link, then click it in preview mode to make sure it opens the correct location.

steps.heading: Share your finished invitation
steps.body: Export or share the invitation as a link so the location stays clickable when guests view it.

faq.question: Will the location link work if I print the invitation?
faq.answer: No, clickable links only work in digital formats — for print, consider adding a QR code instead.

faq.question: Can I add a QR code linking to the location instead?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva has a built-in QR code generator you can add alongside or instead of a text link.

faq.question: Does the link work when shared as a PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, hyperlinks remain clickable in PDF exports as long as they're viewed digitally.

faq.question: Is adding a link a paid Canva feature?
faq.answer: No, adding hyperlinks to invitations is available on Canva's free plan.
